title: NOT WATER PROOF, otherwise meh
text:
Why the eff would you have a swim function on a watch that isn't water resistant?!!
I'm so mad. I barely even tested it, I didn't even go for a real swim. Return window is closed.
If it didn't HAVE A SWIM function, I would never have tried. The charging is all external, no ports. What a stupid glaring oversight. Not even like missing the "e" in "femal" cycle tracking, which is also pretty dumb. They *made* me trash the device.
Otherwise, it worked fine. I wish there were more faces or that I could customize that. I also wish the fitness screens could be bypassed without ending function.

title: DONT BUY IT
text: [[VIDEOID:ab1be5b8aa9793214d0b808dda22b7cf]] Completely stopped working I will say within 20 days of purchase. Yes, it was within the return window but I was in middle of a cross country move and just thought I forgot to charge it or something. Basically after 15+ days of not paying it attention I charged it and it showed the charging sign. After 3 hours if charging I wore the watch and realized it doesn't work. I could not reconnect it to the app. Just blank screen. The only time screen works is when I charge it. I tried to contact seller and I was told someone will contact me. That hasn't happened. The app is not connecting to the watch and I'm unable to get a refund. I wanted a cheap non branded watch and was confused between this and yamays. Idk how yamay is but go for that one cause this is waste of money. Oh yeah, in the two weeks it did work. Sleep tracking never worked. Period tracker has a mind of its own. If you put in a 28 day cycle it will automatically start your period on

title: Cute, inexpensive, does what I need it to do
text: [[VIDEOID:1282f843db2fd51089b9a36048b586d1]] I have had this watch for over a month. I bought the smart watch to trach my steps/miles, tell time, and to see my messages when my phone is in my purse. This watch does the job. It has a long battery life too. I charge it maybe once per week. I exchanged the pink rubber band for a black magnetic metal one. It makes the watch look a little more chic. I'm glad I bought it and would recommend it. I've not figured out the sleep tracking and some of the other features. Note: this watch will not do all that your Samsung or Apple watch will do, but if you want the basics at 1/5 the cost, this one is great.

title: You Get What You Pay For
text: I bought this "smart" watch because of all the reviews that said it was comparable in features to the name brands (Samsung/Apple/FitBit/Garmin) and I was curious so I got one to use and test out for a couple of weeks. Believe me when I tell you, IT IS NOT even close to comparable!!!  There is a reason why the name brands are more expensive: they do a heck of a lot more than this watch. While this watch is very pretty, it is also very basic and limited, which is fine if you are an average smartwatch user who only requires telling the time, steps counting and notifications (if you can even get them to work on this thing). People also bragged about the battery life, which I admit is very good. However, the battery life is good because the watch doesn't really do anything. While I admit that my Galaxy watch needs to be charged every night, I am willing to make that compromise because I am a power user and have been spoiled by my Galaxy Watches. So this watch, even with good battery life, d

title: It's a smart watch!!
text:
WOW.

No but really! It's a decent watch! If you're on the like. Teenager end of phone ownership (phownership??) then this is a solid starter to get you introduced to the concept.
If you're on the older end and just want something to feel somewhat caught up with the times, this kinda does the trick!
For me it's just too much information. I have a casio digital that tells me what time it is, date and weekday and that's plenty.

The sleep tracking was neat and illuminating to my sleep habits and being able to get my vitals on tap is pretty dope. But for the effort and worry of "Will this chip, will the break, oh no water, take it off" it doesn't really hit the right nerve for me. Cheap $15 digital can go through white water rapids and be good. Or gone. Either way I have a backup waiting in the wings. :)

IN SUMMATION: If you'd like to get a smartwatch to see if it's up your alley, I really would recommend this. I'm giving mine to a friend who expr

title: Great device for the price!
text:
I was a little unsure about this item, but I’m glad I bought it. The two main functions I wanted were step tracking and activity/water reminders. These two functions have been great, but I am pleasantly surprised by the watch overall. It looks nice and holds a charge forever. I haven’t recharged it again yet and I got it a 8 days ago. I do like the many activity tracking options. You won’t get everything but there is a good spread of activities, including yoga! I like that I can see a text and decide if I want to deal with it. Love checking the weather and my HR. Was surprised by the BP option and it seems accurate. I didn’t get all scientific or anything but it reads what I would expect. I run very low and that’s what I’m getting from the watch. For the price I am very happy.

For cons, it’s not super fancy or overloaded with options. The app is ok. Again, you’re not going to get a lot of options or tracking info. As a matter of fact, it doesn’t even do anything with your ex

title: It's just okay
text:
I've had this watch for a few days and here's my honest review:
I work outside with animals and wanted a waterproof watch that would notify me about incoming calls and display received texts so I can determine if I need to step away from work to use my phone or not.
All in all I will continue to use it, but I do plan to invest in something more suitable for my preferences within the next year.

Pros: it synced up to my phone with no problem, the band is widely adjustable for any wrist size, it is waterproof, and easy to understand the functions, and the watch face can be customizable with pictures from your phone. The battery life is impressive!
Cons: about 25% of the time it doesn't notify me when i get a text or call (which is the reason i bought it, so i wouldn't have to pull my phone out at work so this is a huge con for me), the brightness level isn't really bright enough if you are outside in the sun, and it's not exactly a "smart" watch; for example I e

title: Great watch!
text: This was my first smart watch.  I have small wrists, and I found the watch face to be very large, but definitely smaller than others I've seen.  I received a Fitbit Versa 2 as a gift and the face is a little bit smaller and lighter BUT I liked the interface and app of this watch way better.  It's easier to use.  I had lots of connectivity problems with fitbit, but none with this watch.  It works great.  Long battery life.  Very easy to use.  My only wish, is that the face was thinner and smaller diameter, other than that, just as good, if not better, than your higher priced name brand smart watches for a fraction of the price.  Highly recommend.  Picture shows watch on right compared to Fitbit Versa 2 on left.

title: A very good watch *for the price*
text:
[[VIDEOID:ae096372f7e47ca397de134d7d1e987]] I spent weeks and weeks searching for the best bang on a budget. And this is it. For $39 I’m not sure you could get a better basic smartish watch. I say that because it is fairly basic.
There’s about 3 watch faces to choose from on the watch, then about 20 on the app. However their all slightly unattractive options. And there’s no way to download more or customize them. Which is kinda a bummer.
Beyond that it does the tracking and you’re able to check weather and change songs.
It only works when your phone is connected by Bluetooth.
There’s no passcodes or personal information on the watch so if it were to get lost or stolen you’re just out $39
The watch seems to be decent quality however the screen scratches pretty easily.
I wouldn’t get this as a replacement for Samsung or Apple watches.
But for the budget watch this is the only one worth getting

title: Very limited life span
text: Too costly for what is now a disposable watch.  Within about 3 months the watch started randomly sending two vibrating signals like it was trying to make a notification of some sort.  Second vibration came with a short screen request to shut down the watch.  Side button quit working after about a month but was not an issue.  Still kept accurate time, fairly good step counts, and notifications of incoming texts and phone calls.  Just can’t stand the frequent and random vibrating. Unlike other commenters my watches battery life was very good.

title: Excellent quality! Easy to use!
text: After having a recent Afib event, the doc at the hospital told me to get my mom an Apple watch to monitor her heart rate. After hearing that my sister's watch crapped out on her. I turned to Amazon. I found this watch and researched it. Great ratings! Even greater price! Mom is 80 and can navigate the settings with ease. I highly recommend this watch to everyone!! Now to get one for myself!!!

title: A great battery life….
text: [[VIDEOID:88a265075d036217cb90c0279c96df75]] The battery life on this watch is amazing. I can go four days to five days without having to charge it. The screen is better to see and the backlight is just right not to bright. The alarm vibrates on my wrist to wake up, not an annoying buzzer or ring. I’m so satisfied with this product. Thinking about getting one for my mom.

title: Amazing battery life!
text: Love this watch and don't regret choosing this one. I actually returned a Samsung Galaxy watch after one day because the battery life was awful. This watch has a LOT of the same features but has an absolutely AMAZING battery life. I charged it completely prior to set up and wearing and it literally lasted a full 13 days before needing to charge. Measures heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen level very accurately. I am a nurse and have used professional equipment to verify the accuracy. The ONLY con I found, and reason I didn't give 5 starts is that the app made changes to the functionality of my Samsung Galaxy phone making it difficult to answer calls. It changed my caller ID screen, how I answer or ignore calls, and the phone screen wouldn't show like normal while talking, then had to open notifications to hang up the phone.  I tried turning off the call/text notification to the watch but that did not fix the problem. I uninstalled the app and phone went back to working like normal.

title: Doesn't hold up well
text: I like the features, but this amount of band discoloration after a few days, wearing lighter colored fabric is unacceptable. Based on similar reviews, I'm clearly not the only one. I reached out to company with my order number, and no response. I really like the rose gold dial, but wish they had the option with a black band.

title: It stopped for no reason twice
text: I am returning the first one that I got because I think perhaps there may be a flaw in it because for no reason at all it just shut itself off or wouldn't show me any information on the watch and it did that to me twice so when it happened again today the second time I decided I'm returning it and requesting a replacement if it happens with the replacement then I'm getting my refund. I don't like sleeping with it on it's uncomfortable not used to sleeping with any kind of jewelry I did use it a few days and checked my sleep and it seemed to give me the same answer every time so I don't know if that's very accurate either? I realize these gadgets are not accurate however I'm just trying to find a way to motivate myself to move around more and I refuse to pay over $100 for one of these smart watches so I got this one on a lightning deal.

title: not as advertised
text:
the watch face does not come on when you raise your arm or turn or flick your wrist. this is a major problem if you assistive devices for walking.
the advertised picture is not available as a watchface when setting up the watch.
this watch is very heavy.
the band is inflexible plastic, not silicone-like at all.

this product is going back.

title: It hurt my wrist
text: I had high hopes for this watch. Did not pair it with my phone as I just wanted a watch to tell time and track my steps. The band was difficult to install, and once I put it in, within minutes it made my wrist ache from the contact. There doesn’t seem to be a way to set the time without pairing through the phone app. Not what I expected. Returning this now.

title: Very good value for a Smartwatch!
text: Bought this for my wife to monitor heart and BP concerns. Paired up easily with her Samsung Android phone. Seems fairly accurate when compared to her regular (commercial) monitoring device. Battery life exceptionally good, runs at least a week, we charge it routinely once per week, each Sunday. Sleep tracking is very helpful, use watch display for quick readouts, mobile app for more expanded details including actual times of sleep and type of sleep.  The only slightly negative aspect of this watch is the plastic band provided. It's a little loose and sloppy. In summary, a great little device at a very good $47 price compared to the big name watches at $300. Highly recommend it.

title: Exceptional battery life - Fitbit comparison below
text:
I'm not sure how great it is at reading heart rate...did some heart pumping cardio and it didn't change much. The blood pressure and oxygen saturation don't appear to be accurate either. I put in on a family member who was ill and using a professional blood oxygen meter and it said he was at 98% and was admitted to the hospital for issues with blood oxygen 2 days later.
The step count matched my Fitbit, I did check it a few times before selling the old Fitbit. I like the many sports features where you can record walking, elliptical, etc, but while you're in record mode you can't use your watch for anything else, which kind of stinks if you're walking on a treadmill and want to set a timer, or change your music, or look at anything else. This does not record "active minutes" like Fitbit.
I do like how it displays text messages, and if a call comes in you can reject it (not sure what would happen if you tried to answer) from the watch.
Even with all the above, it's a great

title: Does not track steps well.
text: Does not track steps very well. When cutting grass I walked about 2500 steps and it recorded 350 or so. Also at park and walking it shorts my steps. I have a lot of experience with $50 and below smart watches. Cheaper watches track steps better, but I have to add the app is good and watch faces are easy to read. The battery is best lasting battery I have had on smartwatch, but my experience with these watches after 3 months the batteries start to drain fast and need replacing. I am still happy with watch and not returning. I would highly recommend if not using for step tracking. Hope this helps.

title: Do not buy it!!
text: I've had this for awile.. it hardly connects to my phone unless I'm less than 3 ft away. And now the lazer has started burning my arm. I have burn marks. I figured I would switch arms just in case it was fluke and sure enough the same damn thing happened.  Not worth it.. look at investing in a better watch. Waterproof and easier to charge and clean. The charger would constantly not connect.  The Bluetooth constantly shut off as well. So I would have to reboot the app.

title: You get what you pay for, if that.
text: Sleep tracking doesn’t work right, the mobile app ehh, you can’t find the same things that are on your watch on the app. You can’t go very far from it or it disconnects, I am saying lucky if four feet. I can go from one room to the other and it disconnects. I thought I could use it for when I was using my stationary bike, NOPE! About the only thing it’s good for is what time it is, but watch fast it doesn’t stay on that long! The longest you can get the face to stay on is 9 seconds! Very disappointed in this, I wasted my money on something that I could have just bought a regular watch!

title: started off ok
text: watch started off ok but as i used it i noticed everything about it wasnt all that great. features it claimed to have were subpar and the overall use was disappointing. I returned for refund.

title: Worked great for about a week and then...
text:
Update: I talked with customer service and I guess it needs a 30 second button hold to reset. So if your screen ever goes blank, try plugging it in and holding down the button for 30 seconds.

I need a simple to use "smart" watch for work. I can't have my volume or vibrate on on my phone but need to be able to know when someone is calling/texting at all times because being available constantly is part of my job. I really don't want to splurge on an expensive Samsung watch so decided to give this a try first.

I loved it for the first week or so. I was finally able to keep up with all of the texts and calls without my phone making noise constantly.

However, the screen went blank completely and I figured the battery was dead but I can't seem to revive it. The screen shows that it's charging and I've let it charge for a while now. I can't get it to turn back on. I've tried holding the button down for 10+ seconds and have tried a combo of button taps. I've ro

title: There's way better smart watches on Amazon! This watch isn't it!
text:
I'm gonna start off my review by saying I'd give this watch and the seller Linking Port, a complete 0 star rating if I could! From what I thought, was a very nice watch, turned out to be completely junk! Not Amazon's fault because Amazon helped me 100%! Thanks Amazon!
But the seller, Linking Port, didn't help at all! The face of the first watch scratched easily as said below. The seller Linking Port, tried to give me way less than what I actually paid for the whole entire watch, way less, trying to cover their mess about giving me $10 as a refund to buy a screen protector! Utter ridiculous! Watch is total junk, seller is junk and after receiving the replacement, it wouldn't even charge at all! Oh and one more thing, sleep tracking doesn't work after 12am! So if you need or want to track your sleep after 12am, you're pretty much out of luck with this watch! There's better ones on Amazon, but this watch isn't it. Why? Because I already had 2 of them and not trying a 3rd! After recei

title: Tiene problemas el sofwear
text: Tiene problemas en el software del equipo. Un día funciona desplazar en la pantalla y otro día no. Un día funciona encender la pantalla al subir la mano y otro día no. Otro día no funciona nada y de nuevo vuelve a funcionar....y asi.... no sirve.

title: 1 year good
text:
[[VIDEOID:43810f2d34d8f4e697f7fd7209bf574d]] 5 to 3

Updat May 6,2022 ..it just started to glitch and hard to see the screen was working a few minutes ago less than 20mins or so.. 1yrs on the dot !!

title: Band is tearing...where you tighten it...in a week?
text: Band is tearing up in a week...where you tighten it

title: Ok buy
text: Well it would have been a great buy for me but I had to end up giving it to my daughter because my skin obviously is too dark for the watch to actually read my blood pressure or anything like that things like that sleep tracker doesn't work on me my daughter's skin is a bit lighter so it can pick up and actually work for her so if you have darker skin tone especially if you're African American I would not suggest this watch.

title: Not accurate
text: It's difficult to use and doesn't keep accurate information on activities, steps or sleep.  Don't depend on the alarm to wake you.  Never know when the battery is low until its too late

title: Great little smart watch
text: Does a lot of functions.  Must be pretty close to phone for app to work. Holds a charge for at least 5 or 6 days.

title: Long Battery Life - Excellent Customer Sercice
text: Received the watch the very next day but the charger was missing from the pkg. Got ahold of their customer service and they shipped not 1 but 2 chargers by the next day. Love the watch, looks great and works just as well as my fitbit that i replaced, & for a lot less $$.  Very happy with my purchase!

title: I have 4 smart watches .
text: I have 4 smart watches all over 2 hundred bucks  purchased  this just see if it works as described  ..turns out it does and also does everything my other watches are capable of doing ..except maybe playing the music and accessing social media ..but overall it's a great stylish  smart watch ..how ever I changed the band didn't like the big rubber band that came with it ..it also got dirty within days so it had to go but it was easy to change the band now I don't have any complaints I've even purchased my mom one and convinced my daughter to purchase one and they love them.

title: Horrible!!!!
text: The interface is in Russian and the app doesn't work...the product is JUNK!

title: Set up was a breeze
text: Just recieved this today and really like the look and the feel of the band. Set up was so easy. Have a fitbit now and found this to be much easier to set up. The phone finder feature alone is worth getting this watch. Anxious to see how the fitness tracking compares!

title: Would be nice of all the functions worked.
text: Stopped giving me notifications of text messages the second day I had it. Iv checked permissions, made sure its connected, restarted and reset..  It just won't work anymore. Disappointing

title: Just A Glorified  DUMB Watch
text:
I saw great reviews so decided to get this smartwatch. It DOES have a great battery life, had it almost a week without having ti charge it. With that being said, the rest of the features are pretty much crap. For instance, went to the ER for kidney stones and because I was in SO much pain, my blood pressure shot up to 170/103! I went to my watch to see if it matched. It was normal 126/80... REALLY?!
Then I checked the weather on my watch today. It said sunny and 48. It's been raining ALL DAY and in the upper 50s.
So NONE of these measurements are even the slightest bit close!
Looks like I got scammed.😵‍💫

title: Cute but low functionality
text: The watch looks super nice and I loved the round watch face. There were an okay number of watch face designs to choose from. Sleep tracking was very rudimentary. The alarm (one of the main reasons I wanted a smartwatch) was kind of worthless in that it would vibrate a set number of times, then turn itself off. Not super helpful if you want to be sure you’re awake before the vibrating stops. 🤨 I ultimately returned it.

title: Great
text: Love the large print. Easy to charge. My battery lasted 7 days before I had to charge

title: Great value for a great watch!
text: Great watch. I am shocked by the quality and accuracy. Best piece of jewelry I have ever bought!

title: great watch and more UPDATE
text: I still love this watch but I want to add a comment- the weather feature does not update when it is synced, and the info is not current. Big disappointment but i still love everything else about it. It also tells time!

title: This is a BIG watch, and doesn't work very long
text: 11/21 The watch holds a charge for about two weeks!!! It's a good working watch, does it's thing, but the face is so big it gives me bruises on my wrist and hand. I sleep in it, and at times the band won't stay on and I have to look for it in the morning. 01/10/22 UPDATE: I can no longer get the watch to sync, and cannot change settings. Piece of junk and they won't refund money

title: Nice... while it lasted
text: I got this watch in February 2022, primarily to track my activity and help with motivating me to move more. In that regard it worked well... while it lasted. The different sports modes were mostly accurate (walking etc.). However, now it's June and the screen has stopped working. It no longer lights up when I flick my wrist, or press the button, thus the watch is not useless. Really really disappointing. I know it was not as expensive as some higher end watches, but it wasn't exactly cheap. Would not recommend, unless you want a decent watch only for a few months.

title: wife loves it
text: She's always looking at her wrist now. She likes it better than all her others she has. I think it looks too big on her.

title: It's perfect 💜
text: I absolutely love everything about this watch. From the color, to the fit, to the size of the face... This watch literally does it all and for a little over $35??? You can't beat it. The only thing I haven't seen that it can do yet is answer the phone or actually text from the watch. But I'm in love with it. It's gorgeous and it's techy and functional.

title: Awesome Watch
text: This wasy first smart watch and I absolutely love it. My husband, who is not new to them, even had to get one for himself when he saw mine. Very highly recommend!

title: Sleep Function doesn’t work
text: Sleep monitor function is very unstable, I could use only like less than a week, it then never works well

title: Some functions don’t work right.
text: I like heart rate, and blood pressure.  Steps for miles is definitely off and sleep as well.

title: Works great for silent alarm
text: I get asked all the time if this is an Apple Watch. I bought it only for the vibrating alarm, and that is flawless - I’ve never set an alarm and then later it doesn’t actually ring (unlike Fitbit whose app was super finicky). Plus the battery on this lasts weeks. I can get calls & texts on my watch, but most of the time I have that turned off so I don’t get woken up at night. Overall very happy!

title: Wow! This Freaking Watch!
text: At first I was skeptical. How could a 50 dollar smartwatch do all that? You can't even get a Fitbit for that price. Well people-- I was wrong. Living in the 21st century has its benefits. Like spending a tenth of the price for that fancy watch you've been wanting! This thing has a damn good battery life, amazing response on the display, and actually accurate readings. The display is customizable to any picture you want, which is one of my favorite features. I connect it to my phone and can read text messages and even emails and social media if I wanted. It has alarms, weather, EVERYTHING! I'm so happy with my watch. You wont regret it!

title: ABOVE AVERAGE FOR THE PRICE
text: I replaced the samsung galaxy with this watch. I did not use all the features on the samsung so my husband took it. I chose this one, love the over sized face, easy to read. its pretty and the battery life is fantastic. I do have to reconnect it frequently to the wifi.  I do not think it is always accurate on heart rate, steps, bp but I'm not concerned about that or I'd kept the Galaxy. I'm medical and have those devices for health reasons. The one thing that I can't figure out, when it's connected to my android, samsung phone and the phone rings, it will ring on the watch but I can never answer on the watch. I can hang up so did I miss that it wasn't for talking on? Regardless, for the price, I like it

title: Not as great as I’d hoped
text: It seems fairly easy to use, set up wasn’t hard, but trying to bind it to my email is proving impossible. I keep getting the code from them, but when I enter that it tells me the code is wrong . . .  And I can’t seem to sync it with the actual weather, it’s always 20 deg. f or c. I’ve gone through the whole reboot restart process and it’s not changing. There are 2 sleep tracking times, one shows a few hours, the other shows more. Is this showing REM? Who knows because it’s not made very clear. I also can’t seem to sync it with my noon app. Sad.

title: Doesn't Always Work
text:
Pros:
-Cheaper than really expensive smart watches
-A beautifully designed selection of watchfaces you can download for free
-Message and call notifications
-Weather
-Easily play, pause and stop music as long as your smartphone is already playing it
-The app you download is actually pretty helpful, but could use more features
-Great battery life!

Cons
-Doesn't track steps accurately - one morning I woke up and put the watch on and before I even stepped out of bed it had tracked 8 steps...
-Sedentary reminder goes off when standing up and moving
-Range is VERY short - I can be in the kitchen and have my phone in the dining room right next to the kitchen and will not get notifications. There is no wall or door between the rooms, it is open space.
-Most other popular fitness apps do not connect and read data from the app included with the watch "FitCloud Pro" so I still have to manually enter information into my fitness app

title: You get what you pay for: cheap and defective
text: The watch was so cute and the size was perfect. The functionality seemed great. I had high hopes for this watch. However, it didn’t work as advertised. I tried over and over to get it to sync, receive notifications from my phone, and just work in general. Nothing seemed to work but the date/time and allowing me to change the clock face picture. Perhaps I got a dud/defective watch, but I’m not taking chances with a new one. I requested a full refund.

title: Fantastic customer service
text: Bought for my bfs mom this past Christmas. She loves it and has had no issues at all. She cracked the screen and it lost touch function. There is a 1 yr manufacturers warranty and they honored it. I emailed them and they requested a picture of the screen and after receiving that they verified my address and immediately shipped a new one out. The entire process from me emailing them to me receiving the replacement took less than a week. I couldn't be happier with this seller.

title: Great price fitness tracker
text: Great watch for the price. Fitness tracker with several choices for sport activities, heart rate, weather, music, timer, stopwatch and blood pressure. It will alert when I have a phone cal, but can not get it to sync up for text message alerts. You can change your watch face to fit your style.

title: Good looking watch, don't like skin marks it leaves...
text:
I loved this watch. Love the color, love the features, love the look. DIDN'T love what it did to my wrist. Every couple of days I had to change how I wore it because it would leave marks on my skin that was similar to a rash. The marks never hurt, like some reviews I read, just didn't look good on my skin.
I eventually stopped wearing it all together after the band broke less than a year after purchase. I have thought about getting another band to wear it again, I just haven't because i don't want those marks on my skin again, I don't know what is causing it, it is coming from the charging points on the watch itself though...

title: Large screen size.
text: It's very large.  Screen is wider than my wrist.  I am terrible at paying attention to measurements so it's my fault.  Seems good otherwise but not sure I will be able to wear it

title: This is a GREAT watch!
text: No, I cannot answer the phone or texts when I receive them, BUT I am always notified by my watch that they are there. I am not much on answering on something so little, so this watch and it's features are GREAT for me!! The price was right and it is quite stylish. Took a bit getting use to the size of it, but now I am use to it and really enjoy it!! I would consider buying another one in the future if I needed one again. It fits comfortably on my wrist and I get a lot of compliments on it! The price was good as well since I am too cheap to pay all outdoors for the other ones that I think aren't worth that much money.... It does what I need and want it to, nothing more, nothing less. Would HIGHLY recommend!!

title: Decepcionada
text: No puedo leer mis mensajes en especial mala calidad pues diría que es por el precio pero en la descripción te venden muchas funciones que mo trabajan, apenas me duro un mes y dejo de funcionar

title: Not impressed--clock always switches to between modes--walking mode never stayed on not reliable
text: i made a mistake choosing this product switching between screens never tracked sleep,walking,sports and exercise time

title: Easy to sync, love this watch
text: I just received this watch today. So easy to set up. My Samsung S2 would not keep a charge despite everything I did. So I started searching for a new smart watch. I read many, many reviews on this watch. The price was phenomenal  so I thought I would try it! So far its just what I wanted. Easy, stylish. I bought the black one, but switched out bands to a turquoise one. So happy with it!

title: Not for fitness tracking
text: Not for fitness tracking. Had it and wore it for 3 days. Told me I was burning 500 calories a day with 20 min workouts and walking the dogs multiple times a day. Ended up returning it.

title: I love it!!!
text: I love the watch it is so helpful in so many ways!!!!

title: Complicated
text: I sent this back because it is very complicated to operate. Plus, I don't own a smartphone or Bluetooth so it is very limited at best.

title: Love this!
text: My mother got me this watch for my birthday and I absolutely love it. The battery life is phenomenal, I love the ability to change the screen/wallpaper, it keeps up with my calls and messages so I never miss anything, and it does a great job tracking my steps. It is a great alternative to the more expensive watches out there. Definitely worth it.

title: didnt last a year wont charge or turn on
text: Bought 12-5-21.  It is now 11-12-22 and the watch will not come onor charge.  I wore it a few days ago hung it up as always. Would not come on So I  tried to charge it.  Still did not come on so charged it again last night. still not coming on and won't sync with my phone.  would not buy again.  I do expect a watch to last over a year  I paid over $ 60.  Dec 2021

title: Still on the fence
text:
I’m still on the fence on this one. I’ve used similar brands before and I don’t like how it track sleeps because it just keeps track of it during “bedtime hours” so any naps during the day didn’t count. This was one feature I really want it to auto recognize without having to be within a window.

The other down side that I don’t like is that you have to set your exercise. My previous Fitbit auto recognize my exercise without having to push a button. It kept track of my heart rate within that exercise time. With this watch it won’t keep up with the exercise or the fluctuated heart rate unless you have it set to the exercise.

Other than that you pay for what you get. It a bit more manual but it a nice looking watch with a bigger screen.

title: This thing is massive
text: Seems to be a well made watch. But This watch is just too big for me, and I’m not a petite or even small woman. Also the watch faces don’t cover the entire surface of the watch. And the lack of functions compared to another watch I own is too obvious. I don’t even use the exercise functions yet, so I’m talking basics here.  I guess for the price it was fine, but it’s going back.

title: Smart purchase
text: This is one of the smartest purchases I have ever made. This watch does everything I want it to and more. It is amazing with a great price point. this watch tracks steps, tracks workouts, has a timer and a stopwatch, has music control, period tracker, find my phone feature and notifies me of incoming messages and calls. Along with the standard things listed on the add.

title: Buy it!! You won't regret it!
text: If you are trying to be healthy and enjoy walking or running this is a must! It counts your steps and the battery life is great.  Plus it looks cute too.

title: Nice but is it Worth It?
text:
Once delivered and opened up I was very impressed with the packaging and the watch itself feeling so high quality. But the interface just aint it, scrolling though ot was slow and you can see the lag.

It really seems like a decent watch but I was just not a fan of the interface and how it functions. Watch doesn't display, the slowness, and quality of the lcd just didn't impress me enough to actually want to hold on to this watch.

I have a Samsung gear s3 which is bulky but fits me perfectly with a better interface and more features than what this watch had to offer.

I would only recommend it if you just want it for aesthetics, since I feel like you can find the same watch with the same quality but better features.

title: Great value
text: My daughter loves it. Works great and easy to use

title: worth WAY more than the cost
text: This watch is worth more than the sale price! So many features, and I just love it. There is no way I'd spend $200 on anything else with this on the market! I have had it all Summer and it is now Oct, still new condition, I wear it all the time, and I can adjust all kinds of things on the phone app. All of the health monitoring is accurate, and useful for steps, BP, oxygen levels, so many things.

title: Look exactly like the photo
text:
Love how clasy these look.
They track sleep, like the timer and the drink water reminder. I love the bp,BP, and o2 readings though

Cons,
can't send, reply, or call from it. Can't download any apps.. whatever it comes with it has.

title: Bought this for my mom. She loves it
text: I bought this smart watch for my mom because she likes to walk every day at the park and she counts her steps. It’s very easy to use  My mom is 73 yrs old and she loves it.

title: Really good choice 👌🏻
text: Firstly i bought this smartwatch for my husband as a father’s day gift and i had bought another brand for myself. But mine was too heavy for my wrist that’s why i returned it and ordered this smartwatch. I am really happy with the smartwatch. The app is very easy to set up and connet any android phone. I only don’t like the plastic bands, i’ve ordered metal bands

title: Looks great, but don't buy
text: My fitbit died, so I wanted to replace it ASAP with something that was more affordable. I did quite a bit of research and read MANY reviews on different watches. I was sold on this one. Packaging was good. Came safely. The watch itself felt great, looked great, but wasn't completely in love withbgyr functionality. The battery life I wasn't impressed with at all and the charger it comes with is an awful design. It quit charging the 2nd time I needed to. I dont advise to purchase this watch at all.

title: Text notifications flawed
text: I don't receive notifications about texts from ALL contacts, yet I get notified about all phone calls regardless..? Returning it!

title: Great budget semi-smart watch
text:
4mos later update: I still think this is worth the $40ish dollars I paid for it. Battery is still awesome. Watch still SUCKS at the find my phone option.

Original Review: I was spoiled. I had a Fossil smart watch that loved but I killed it. I couldn't afford to replace it but I like having a watch and a pedometer. After much searching I went with this one. It stays charged for 2 weeks at a time and is perfect for my basic needs except 1 thing. It disconnects from my phone of they are more than 10 feet apart. Which sucks if you have ADHD and need to use the "find my phone" feature of the watch.
Sleep tracking is spot on.
Mobile app is basic but so is the price.

title: Great Watch!!
text: The battery is great much better than my last one. The time is very accurate.

title: Don’t
text: This watch buzzes all of the time. Buzzes so much it runs the battery down WHILE it’s trying to charge. I’ve even turned all of the alerts off and it STILL buzzes😂 The face is huge. Would not recommend.

title: Does what I needed for a much cheaper price than an Apple Watch
text: Love this watch! I had been looking for months and just wouldn’t spend the money for an Apple Watch on myself. This is super pretty and elegant and beyond the sleep tracking it’s been accurate. I refuse to believe I only sleep 5 hours a night. That’s just not feasible. I reset it several times and that part has never lived up but everything else is great!

title: Calidad  y precio
text: Uso personal, esta muy bien el.reloj, lo que no me gusto  es que cuando lo compre ofrecian los protectores  de pantalla y los.compre tambien. Pero son muy pequeños . No deberian ofrecerlos  por que no es la referencia para este reloj

title: So far so good!
text:
I've only had it 3 days and so far I'm happy with it. You have to be close to your phone so you get text/call notifications. Battery life is great I charged it once.

Edit.... The sleep tracking only works at night.. "normal sleeping hours"

title: Nice, affordable watch
text: Wife likes nice watches so is not a huge fan of smart watches but does like to have one to wear around the house when not wearing one of her nice ones. This one hit the bill and she can get other bands for it. Battery life is about one week which is fine and tracks steps well and notifies when calls or texts are coming in. Can change faces to so she’s happy with it for what it is.

title: Hard to learn.
text: Still trying to figure it out. Very useful, sleep track not accurate.

title: Nice
text: I love the timer and health features of this watch. The only downside is, once the phone and watch are connected, your phone does not unlock automatically for phone calls. I have missed many calls trying to unlock my phone and answer it.

title: Love it
text: I love this watch. It has a long battery life. Very pretty. Easy to use. I love it. I would compare it to the Samsung watch but much cheaper.

title: Nice looking but
text: In general, the watch is nice looking but the screens you can swipe to are very limited and so far no matter what I do, can't change Celsius to Fahrenheit even in the app.

title: Good watch, lasted a year
text: Watch is pretty good, lasted a little more than a year. Did have to buy a couple of new watch straps in between. After about 13 months, woke up one day and had lines through the screen. Bizarre, as no hits, drops or damage occurred prior. Would still but another though, for the price a year is pretty good.

title: was able to connect to iPhone,,,can check watch HR, BP, Ox...
text: information is not always correct

title: Great watch for the money‼️
text: The only thing I don't like is the strap. It won't stay adjusted, it's uncomfortable gets caught on everything and comes out of the keeper.

title: Maybe worth it to some…
text: The faces don’t smoothly change from one to another. My watch seemed to trip up quite a bit when doing so. It also has limited sports modes which is why I’ll actually be returning it. It’s a shame because I love the style!

title: Nice watch but...
text: I would have kept this watch as it is very nice and clicks all the bells and whistles. However, I was disappointed to see that the mileage is recorded in metric. Everything else recorded in the watch is in English measurement except that. There isn't any setting to change it either. Wish I had known that before my purchase.

title: Very stylish large numbers
text: I like the stylishness of this. Band is a softer in some of the other ones I've had with Fitbit. The battery lasts me for over a week which is fabulous. I don't like that it doesn't track my sleep correctly. If I take a nap it doesn't count as all. Overall I really like the price and I feel this is a good value for my money. It feels pretty feminine for me

title: Lo recomiendo porque es muy barato pero trabaja todo muy bien 👍🏻
text: Me encanta todo trabaja muy bien y lo que más me gusta es que puedo aplicar cualquier fotografía de mi galería 😊

title: Bands comes detached!
text: I love the watch!!  I hate though that the bands needed to be put on!!  I couldn't do it with my fingers!  Found someone to help me thank goodness!

title: Good watch
text: I use as a basic watch to track my steps that’s pretty much it I wanted a different style from my iPhone watch. It’s good as basic watch and are counter. It does give heart rate and etc but I don’t really mess with it. The battery lasts me about a week (I turn the watch off every night after 8-9 hour day at work). I did scratch the screen on something which sucks but it doesn’t affect the watch function at all. It was delivered in good time as well.

title: Poor directions that made no sense.  Unable to get it to work.
text: Directions wrong.  Doesn't function.
